revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a 64x64px pixel-art terrain tile sheet for game development, arranged as a strict 4x4 grid of 16 tiles. Each tile is exactly 16x16px. Do not add borders, grid lines, tile IDs, labels, UI, watermark, or text. Final image should read as crisp high-resolution 16-bit SNES-style pixel art: hard pixel edges, no antialiasing blur, consistent palette, one-pixel dark outline where useful, top-left warm light.

Asset profile: snes16_platformer_v1. Preset: world_coastline_16 adapted into a top-down terraced rice paddy water autotile set. Subject: mythical-Japan satoyama tanada rice paddy water tiles. Bright cheerful golden-hour palette with soft blue-and-gold sky reflected in calm mirror-like still water. Use painterly pixel clusters, not flat blue. Water should show soft warm reflections, faint gentle ripples, and thin rows of bright young green rice seedlings. Aze ridges are low mossy earthen banks topped with grass, blending naturally into the water with curved, broken, organic boundaries; avoid hard rectangular bands and avoid straight vertical/horizontal strip patterns.

Cell layout, left to right:
Row 1: four open paddy-water interior variations: mirror water, faint rice rows, subtle ripples, soft sky reflection; tileable and cohesive but not repeated square stamps.
Row 2: water-to-aze edge tiles in order N, S, W, E. Each has mirror water meeting a low grassy earthen ridge only on that side, with curved organic shoreline breakup.
Row 3: outer corner ridge tiles in order NW, NE, SW, SE, with natural curved corners and grass/soil overlap.
Row 4: accent tiles in order: clump of green rice seedlings, lotus leaf, wet stone, muddy bank. Accents remain inside their own 16x16 cells and share the same top-down scale.

Containment: every frame stays inside its own 16x16 cell, same scale, seamless tilemap feel. Tilemap quality: varied texture, edge breakup, lighting drift, no visible checkerboard, no repeated stamp look. World-map material feel: large geographic rice-paddy regions, not close-up realistic village details. Fully opaque where terrain fills the cell; any unused empty space must be solid #FF00FF magenta. Original design only: no third-party characters, logos, likenesses, letter emblems, modern objects, weapons, armor, creatures, horror mood, or dark gloomy palette.
